signals.idea: Fix all 5 architectural concerns in the video-editor effect system: (1) Three effect sources merged silently — make migration path explicit or deprecate legacy DB path, (2) Global singleton registry — make reactive so components always use current registry state, (3) No runtime error boundary around effects — add safety net for buggy/AI-generated custom effects, (4) EffectCreatorPanel preview divergence — make preview composition more faithful to actual timeline rendering, (5) Untyped params — add runtime validation or default-filling from parameterSchema at apply-time. Key files: effects/index.tsx, DynamicEffectRegistry.ts, useEffectRegistry.ts, VisualClip.tsx, TextClip.tsx, EffectLayerSequence.tsx, EffectCreatorPanel.tsx, ParameterControls.tsx, types/index.ts

unresolved_flags.concern: video-editor-effects: EffectErrorBoundary fallback is underspecified; wrapping an effect component cannot recover raw clip content after a render throw unless the boundary also receives an explicit fallback node or equivalent split render path.

video-editor-effects: Reactive registry plan does not guarantee clips re-render against the provider registry because `lookupEffect()` reads the module singleton and `replaceEffectRegistry()` is only called from `useEffect()` after the first render.
effect-param-validation: Plan backfills schema defaults but does not validate wrong-type or invalid-option params against `parameterSchema`, so concern 5 remains only partially fixed.

effect-preview-fidelity: Preview plan keeps hardcoded `PREVIEW_FPS=30` and suggests temporary global registry registration for preview-only code, leaving timing mismatch and shared-state coupling unresolved.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useEffectRegistry.ts:23-50` and `src/tools/video-editor/effects/index.tsx:78-112`. The plan still talks about a 'global singleton' registry, but the repository actually creates a provider-local `DynamicEffectRegistry` with `useMemo()` and only mirrors it into the module singleton via `replaceEffectRegistry()` inside `useEffect()`. That means the real issue is the async registration and global lookup timing, not singleton ownership alone, so the plan does not fully absorb the critique correction.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked `src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts:9-18` and `src/tools/video-editor/components/ParameterControls.tsx:16-40`. The brief asked for runtime validation of missing and wrong params against `parameterSchema`, but the plan's `fillDefaultParams` step only backfills defaults; it does not coerce or reject wrong types, invalid select values, or malformed booleans/colors, so it only partially answers concern 5.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked `src/tools/video-editor/components/EffectCreatorPanel.tsx:139-163`. The brief explicitly called out the preview's hardcoded `320x320` and `30fps` as part of the fidelity gap, but the plan says to keep the `320x320` wrapper and `PREVIEW_FPS`. Using the real wrapping pipeline helps, but leaving preview timing hardcoded means springs and frame-based animations can still diverge from the timeline.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked the proposed boundary shape against `src/tools/video-editor/effects/index.tsx:126-166`. Wrapping `<Continuous>{wrapped}</Continuous>` in an error boundary does not let the boundary render the raw clip content on failure, because the boundary only receives the already-wrapped subtree as `children`. To degrade to 'clip without effect', the API needs an explicit fallback node or a wrapper component that keeps the inner content separate.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked `src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.tsx:19-26`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useEffectRegistry.ts:30-50`, and `src/tools/video-editor/effects/index.tsx:97-169`. Adding `useSyncExternalStore` inside `useEffectRegistry()` is not sufficient by itself, because `VideoEditorProvider` ignores the hook's return value and render-time effect lookup still goes through `getEffectRegistry()`/`lookupEffect()`. Without also bridging the snapshot into the render path, the plan can satisfy the new API shape while clips still render against stale registry state.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked the Step 3 examples against `src/tools/video-editor/effects/index.tsx:139-166`. The plan's sample wrapper text uses `effectName={clip.continuous.type}` for all three categories, but entrance and exit wrappers need `clip.entrance.type` and `clip.exit.type` respectively. As written, the plan's example would log the wrong failing effect name for two of the three paths.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked the preview proposal against `src/tools/video-editor/components/EffectCreatorPanel.tsx:139-163` and `src/tools/video-editor/effects/index.tsx:78-91`. The plan's temporary `'preview-effect'` registry registration would mutate the shared global registry from a local editor preview. That introduces cleanup/lifecycle requirements and cross-component coupling that the current preview path does not have, so the proposed mechanism is not yet technically safe.

Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Searched for effect persistence and found a broader legacy path in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/serialize.ts:96-136`, `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts:198-300`, `src/tools/video-editor/lib/migrate.ts:165-186`, and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineSave.ts:213-218,535-536`. The plan frames consolidation as three sources only, but `customEffects` is still a persisted legacy representation that survives save/load and migration, so the underlying migration problem is broader than the plan states.

Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Searched all `CustomEffectEditor` call sites with ripgrep and found only its own definition in `src/tools/video-editor/components/CustomEffectEditor.tsx`; there are no repo callers. The plan treats this component as active migration surface, but the repository suggests it may be dead code, so migrating it is likely lower leverage than either deleting it or explicitly declaring it out of scope.

Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: Searched all effect lookup/render sites and found `wrapWithClipEffects()` in `src/tools/video-editor/effects/index.tsx:114-169`, `EffectLayerSequence` in `src/tools/video-editor/compositions/EffectLayerSequence.tsx:19-48`, and the global registry accessors in `src/tools/video-editor/effects/index.tsx:78-112`. The plan mentions the render wrappers, but it does not clearly account for the fact that all of them depend on the module singleton, not the hook-local registry object.

Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: Checked `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useEffectRegistry.ts:30-48`. Supporting infrastructure is missing from the plan around the initial registry swap: `replaceEffectRegistry(registry)` happens in `useEffect()`, so the first render of `VisualClip`, `TextClip`, or `EffectLayerSequence` can still hit the lazy default registry before the provider registry is installed. The plan does not say how that first-render gap is closed.

Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: Checked `useEffectRegistry()` callers and found only `src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.tsx:21-26`, where the return value is discarded. That means any plan that relies on returning a reactive registry handle or version from the hook is incomplete unless it also changes the provider or the global lookup path.

Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: Checked all `wrapWithClipEffects()` callers in `src/tools/video-editor/compositions/VisualClip.tsx:223` and `src/tools/video-editor/compositions/TextClip.tsx:45`, plus the separate continuous-effect caller in `src/tools/video-editor/compositions/EffectLayerSequence.tsx:39-47`. The clip wrappers pass `durationInFrames`, `fps`, and category-specific durations, while `EffectLayerSequence` always passes `effectFrames={durationInFrames}` for continuous effects, so the plan needs to preserve those differing caller contracts when adding validation and boundaries.

Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: Checked schema-default callers in `src/tools/video-editor/components/ParameterControls.tsx:35-40`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx:78-92`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/BulkClipPanel.tsx:75-86`, and `src/tools/video-editor/components/EffectCreatorPanel.tsx:185-188,310-365`. The codebase already uses exported `getDefaultValues()` everywhere it needs schema defaults, so exporting the private `getFallbackValue()` just for the new runtime helper would add API surface without handling any caller need that already exists.

Are the success criteria well-prioritized and verifiable?: Checked the `must` criterion 'fillDefaultParams utility exists and merges user params over schema defaults'. That is easy to verify, but it is weaker than the actual requirement to validate params against `parameterSchema`; a change can satisfy this criterion and still let wrong-typed params through unchanged.

Are the success criteria well-prioritized and verifiable?: Checked the `must` criterion 'EffectErrorBoundary exists and catches runtime throws from effect components, rendering children unwrapped on error'. In the current plan this is not a clean yes/no gate, because 'children unwrapped' is ambiguous unless the boundary API explicitly receives separate fallback content rather than only the wrapped effect subtree.

Are the success criteria well-prioritized and verifiable?: Checked the `must` criterion about exposing `subscribe()`/`getSnapshot()`. That criterion verifies API shape, not the actual user-facing behavior that clips re-render after async registration through the global lookup path, so it can pass while the core bug remains.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 30.5. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 21.

warnings: Scope creep detected: the plan appears to be expanding beyond the original idea or recorded user notes.

items.criterion: All three existing test files pass: DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx, useEffectResources.test.tsx, VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx
DynamicEffectRegistry exposes subscribe() and getSnapshot() methods compatible with useSyncExternalStore
Registering an effect via registerAsync triggers subscribed listeners
EffectErrorBoundary exists and catches runtime throws from effect components, rendering children unwrapped on error
wrapWithClipEffects wraps each effect (entrance, exit, continuous) in EffectErrorBoundary
EffectLayerSequence wraps its Effect rendering in EffectErrorBoundary
fillDefaultParams utility exists and merges user params over schema defaults
wrapWithClipEffects calls fillDefaultParams before passing params to effect components
DynamicEffectRegistry stores and retrieves parameterSchema alongside effect components
useEffectRegistry passes parameterSchema from EffectResource when registering resource effects
Built-in effects still take priority over dynamic effects with the same name (existing invariant preserved)
Resource effects take priority over legacy DB effects when names collide
EffectCreatorPanel preview uses wrapWithClipEffects or equivalent wrapping pipeline
New files stay under ~150 lines each (EffectErrorBoundary, fillDefaultParams, useRegistryVersion)
No changes to ClipEntrance/ClipExit/ClipContinuous types (params stays Record<string, unknown>)
Manual smoke test: custom effect with runtime throw degrades gracefully in timeline
Manual smoke test: custom effect with parameters renders correctly with default values

unresolved_flags.concern: video-editor-effects: EffectErrorBoundary fallback is underspecified; wrapping an effect component cannot recover raw clip content after a render throw unless the boundary also receives an explicit fallback node or equivalent split render path.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/effects/index.tsx:126-166` wraps effects as components around `wrapped` content today, so a boundary around that subtree only sees the already-wrapped tree.

unresolved_flags.concern: video-editor-effects: Reactive registry plan does not guarantee clips re-render against the provider registry because `lookupEffect()` reads the module singleton and `replaceEffectRegistry()` is only called from `useEffect()` after the first render.

unresolved_flags.concern: effect-param-validation: Plan backfills schema defaults but does not validate wrong-type or invalid-option params against `parameterSchema`, so concern 5 remains only partially fixed.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts:9-18` defines typed parameter kinds, but the planned helper is described as a merge of defaults over user params rather than a validator/coercer.

unresolved_flags.concern: effect-preview-fidelity: Preview plan keeps hardcoded `PREVIEW_FPS=30` and suggests temporary global registry registration for preview-only code, leaving timing mismatch and shared-state coupling unresolved.
